channel.title: NDTV News Search Records Found 1000
channel.description: NDTV.com provides the latest information from and in-depth coverage of India and the world. Find breaking news, India news, top stories, elections, politics, business, cricket, movies, lifestyle, health, live TV, videos, photos and more.

item.title: 'Arash-e-Kamangir': Weapon Iran Claims To Have Used To Shoot Down US Drone

media:content.media:description: Tehran's Vanak Square shows a billboard bearing an image of legendary mythical Persian archer, Arash

item.description: The new interceptor system is named after a Persian mythology hero who is described in folklore as having fired an arrow to draw the border between Iran and Central Asia.
item.content:encoded: The new interceptor system is named after a Persian mythology hero who is described in folklore as having fired an arrow to draw the border between Iran and Central Asia.

item.title: AI Giant Anthropic Nears $1 Trillion Valuation, Surpasses Rival OpenAI

media:content.media:description: Anthropic's rise came by doubling down on delivering generative AI

item.description: Anthropic's near-trillion-dollar valuation puts it ahead of OpenAI, which was valued at $852 billion in March. Both companies could go public as early as this year.
item.content:encoded: Anthropic's near-trillion-dollar valuation puts it ahead of OpenAI, which was valued at $852 billion in March. Both companies could go public as early as this year.

item.title: 'Indians, Pakistanis Take Our Job': British MP's 'Racist' Remarks Spark Row

media:content.media:description: Lowe claimed British women do not feel comfortable walking down streets taken over by immigrants

item.description: Lowe's remarks soon sparked a controversy, with social media users calling him "racist" and reminding him about Britain's colonial past.
item.content:encoded: Lowe's remarks soon sparked a controversy, with social media users calling him "racist" and reminding him about Britain's colonial past.

item.title: "Highly Inaccurate": Sheikh Hasina On UN Report On Bangladesh Protest Death Count

media:content.media:description: The OHCHR published its report on Bangladesh protests in February last year

item.description: A UN fact-finding report said that up to 1,400 people were killed during the Bangladesh protests in 2024.
item.content:encoded: A UN fact-finding report said that up to 1,400 people were killed during the Bangladesh protests in 2024.

item.title: Japan Airlines Bans Crew From Drinking During Layovers After Flight Delay Incident

media:content.media:description: The airline has suffered major alcohol-related controversies in the past.

item.description: The latest case took place on May 23 when a chief flight attendant scheduled to operate a morning flight from Hiroshima to Tokyo failed mandatory breathalyzer checks before duty.
item.content:encoded: The latest case took place on May 23 when a chief flight attendant scheduled to operate a morning flight from Hiroshima to Tokyo failed mandatory breathalyzer checks before duty.

item.title: On Camera, Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin's New Glenn Rocket Explodes During Test

media:content.media:description: Video from the incident shows smoke emerging from beneath the rocket before it fully combusts

item.description: Florida congressman Mike Haridopolos, whose district includes Cape Canaveral, said in a statement on X that he has been in contact with NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman regarding the explosion.
item.content:encoded: <iframe title="Player" class="head-story__img img__full lazyload" height="300" scrolling=no frameborder=no src="about:blank" data-src="https://www.ndtv.com/video/embed-player/id/1105829/play-via-app-wap?pWidth=100&pHeight=100&embed_type=story&site=classic&autostart=1"> </iframe>Florida congressman Mike Haridopolos, whose district includes Cape Canaveral, said in a statement on X that he has been in contact with NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman regarding the explosion.

item.title: Climate Change Is Making Hajj Pilgrimage Hotter, Less Safe: Study

media:content.media:description: The hajj's timing is governed by the Islamic lunar calendar

item.description: The hajj in western Saudi Arabia, one of the most arid areas on the planet, is one of the world's largest religious gatherings, when hundreds of thousands of pilgrims are exposed to extreme heat.
item.content:encoded: The hajj in western Saudi Arabia, one of the most arid areas on the planet, is one of the world's largest religious gatherings, when hundreds of thousands of pilgrims are exposed to extreme heat.

item.title: How Pigeons Use Iron-Rich Liver Cells To Find Their Way Home

media:content.media:description: Specialized immune cells in the pigeon's liver break down red blood cells and store iron

item.description: Pigeons are a well-known group of frequent flyers that can traverse hundreds of miles (hundreds of kilometers) in a single day.
item.content:encoded: Pigeons are a well-known group of frequent flyers that can traverse hundreds of miles (hundreds of kilometers) in a single day.

item.title: India 'Commits' To Buying $500 Billion Worth Of US Goods. What Experts Said

media:content.media:description: Rubio's visit to India came at a particularly sensitive time in India-US ties

item.description: The concerns gain significance because India is already struggling to preserve its foreign reserves due to a weakening currency and rising oil prices due to the US-Iran war.
item.content:encoded: The concerns gain significance because India is already struggling to preserve its foreign reserves due to a weakening currency and rising oil prices due to the US-Iran war.

item.title: 3 Dead, Including Child, In US Apartment Explosion

media:content.media:description: At least five people also went to hospitals with injuries, Dallas Fire-Rescue spokesperson said

item.description: As dozens of firefighters swarmed to the neighborhood, some residents' friends and relatives worried as they tried unsuccessfully to reach their loved ones
item.content:encoded: As dozens of firefighters swarmed to the neighborhood, some residents' friends and relatives worried as they tried unsuccessfully to reach their loved ones

item.title: "No US Aircraft Shot": Washington Rejects Iran's Claim Of Attack Near Bushehr

media:content.media:description: US-Iran are "very close" to a memorandum of understanding that would extend the ceasefire by 60 days.

item.description: Iran's state TV said early on Friday that a US aircraft was destroyed in Iran's Jam governorate in Bushehr, citing its governor Masoud Tangestani.
item.content:encoded: Iran's state TV said early on Friday that a US aircraft was destroyed in Iran's Jam governorate in Bushehr, citing its governor Masoud Tangestani.

item.title: Watch: JD Vance Chest Bumps Air Force Cadet At Graduation Ceremony, Then A 'Note To Self'

media:content.media:description: Some X users described JD Vance in "bro-mode" at the ceremony.

item.description: After his speech, US Vice President JD Vance greeted members of the graduating class when one cadet surprised him with a playful chest bump.
item.content:encoded: After his speech, US Vice President JD Vance greeted members of the graduating class when one cadet surprised him with a playful chest bump.

item.title: With Oman Warning, Trump Has Now Threatened To Blow Up 1 Out Of Every 13 Countries

media:content.media:description: Not all strikes and threats are of the same nature.

item.description: Trump warned that the US could strike Oman if it tries to take control of the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded: Trump warned that the US could strike Oman if it tries to take control of the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Mount Everest 2026 Sees Record Rush, Over 1000 Climbers Summit Before Closure

media:content.media:description: Mountaineers climbing a slope lined up during their ascent to summit Mount Everest in Nepal.

item.description: The world's highest peak, which straddles the Nepal-China border, can be scaled from either Nepal or the northern face in Tibet. But China closed its route this year.
item.content:encoded: The world's highest peak, which straddles the Nepal-China border, can be scaled from either Nepal or the northern face in Tibet. But China closed its route this year.

item.title: A New Ebola Strain Is Threatening Fresh Outbreak. What Are Doctors Doing

media:content.media:description: Yet even as Ebola spreads, Congo's health crisis continues unabated.

item.description: This is where the Democratic Republic of Congo's latest Ebola outbreak has erupted in the middle of a grinding conflict that has hollowed out public services and displaced millions.
item.content:encoded: This is where the Democratic Republic of Congo's latest Ebola outbreak has erupted in the middle of a grinding conflict that has hollowed out public services and displaced millions.

item.title: Trump Claims Obama, Biden Spent Millions On Reflecting Pool. They Didn't

media:content.media:description: Workers continue to paint the bottom of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool on the National Mall

item.description: Donald Trump had criticised his predecessors' efforts to fix the site at a Cabinet meeting on Wednesday, claiming they spent "hundreds of millions of dollars."
item.content:encoded: Donald Trump had criticised his predecessors' efforts to fix the site at a Cabinet meeting on Wednesday, claiming they spent "hundreds of millions of dollars."

item.title: "She Refused To Pay Drug Dealer": Father Of Indian Student Stabbed In Canada

media:content.media:description: Vidhi, a resident of Borsad in Gujarat, had moved to Canada four years ago.

item.description: Vidhi Megha, an Indian student, was killed on May 15 in St Catharines in Canada, but her family was informed over 10 days later.
item.content:encoded: Vidhi Megha, an Indian student, was killed on May 15 in St Catharines in Canada, but her family was informed over 10 days later.

item.title: 22-Year-Old Student From Gujarat Stabbed To Death In Canada

media:content.media:description: Vidhi Kalpeshbhai Meghani was stabbed earlier this month

item.description: The victim has been identified as Vidhi Kalpeshbhai Meghani, a resident of Borsad in the Anand district of Gujarat.
item.content:encoded: <iframe title="Player" class="head-story__img img__full lazyload" height="300" scrolling=no frameborder=no src="about:blank" data-src="https://www.ndtv.com/video/embed-player/id/1105768/play-via-app-wap?pWidth=100&pHeight=100&embed_type=story&site=classic&autostart=1"> </iframe>The victim has been identified as Vidhi Kalpeshbhai Meghani, a resident of Borsad in the Anand district of Gujarat.

item.title: $300 Billion Investment, Lebanon Solution: Inside 'Almost There' US-Iran Deal

media:content.media:description: US and Iran have reportedly agreed to set up a $300 billion redevelopment fund for Iran.

item.description: The two sides are close to the deal, yet lingering vagaries remain. As the majority of the talks have taken place through the Pakistani and Qatari mediators, it is reportedly not clear whether the Americans and the Iranians have been working on the s
item.content:encoded: The two sides are close to the deal, yet lingering vagaries remain. As the majority of the talks have taken place through the Pakistani and Qatari mediators, it is reportedly not clear whether the Americans and the Iranians have been working on the s

item.title: Aspiring MMA Fighter From Kerala Killed In Rare Bear Attack In Canada

media:content.media:description: Rare bear attack kills Kerala MMA fighter Hrishikesh Koloth working in Saskatchewan.

item.description: Aspiring MMA fighter from Kerala, Hrishikesh Koloth, dies in a rare bear attack at a uranium site in Saskatchewan, Canada.
item.content:encoded: Aspiring MMA fighter from Kerala, Hrishikesh Koloth, dies in a rare bear attack at a uranium site in Saskatchewan, Canada.
